findings.evidence: Line 75 `private abbrev prodFst`; line 265 `theorem prodIso_hom_fst ... prodFst M N = Limits.prod.fst` (no `private`).
findings.fix: Either expose prodFst/prodSnd/prodLift and complete the concrete-object API symmetrically, or make prodIso and its characteristic lemmas private and restate any needed public facts purely via Limits.prod.fst/snd/lift.
findings.issue: Public @[simp] lemmas prodIso_hom_fst/prodIso_hom_snd/prodIso_inv_fst/prodIso_inv_snd (264-298) and prod_lift_comp_prodIso_hom (306) are stated in terms of the `private` declarations prodFst/prodSnd (75,78) and prodLift (81). prodIso is exposed but no public map projects out of the concrete `prod R C M N`, so these characteristic lemmas are unusable through the public API.

findings.evidence: hasBinaryProducts (375)/hasFiniteProducts (379)/hasFiniteBiproducts (399) derive from hasBinaryProduct alone; the prodIso/prodDesc lemmas are not used to build any instance.
findings.fix: Expose only `prod` plus the Has* instances; make prodIso, prodDesc, and the prodIso_* lemmas private unless a concrete downstream consumer is named. If a concrete API is intended, name the consumer and complete it coherently with public projections.
findings.issue: Over-exposed surface: the concrete-product layer (prod, prodIso, prodDesc, prod_lift_comp_prodIso_hom, prodIso_*, prod_fst_inl_add_snd_inr) is beyond what the named roadmap target needs. The target needs the binary/finite product and biproduct instances; downstream then uses Mathlib's generic `M ⨯ N`/`M ⊞ N` API. No consumer is named for the bespoke desc-on-product or the iso-to-concrete-object.

summary: The named target needs only the Has{Binary,Finite}{Products,Biproducts} instances, after which Mathlib's generic prod/biprod API is complete. The extra concrete-object layer (prodIso, prodDesc, prodIso_* lemmas) is speculative surface with no named consumer, and it is internally incoherent: public prodIso/prodIso_*/prod_lift_comp_prodIso_hom are characterized through the private maps prodFst/prodSnd/prodLift, so the iso reaches an object that cannot be decomposed via any public lemma.
